vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure of the expected biome. The pots are present, but the biological specimens (P1-P4) are absent or completely obscured by soil/debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: No foliage visible; White Rabbit anchor is present but isolated.

plant_audit.observations: The pots contain only dark, damp-appearing substrate. No photosynthetic activity or plant structures are detectable in any of the four containers.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and moist across all pots.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: The presence of a white, possibly ceramic or plastic, object (White Rabbit) in the P3 pot is the only non-soil element. No fungal blooms or surface debris noted beyond the substrate itself.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero change in the biological state. The pots have remained in a state of 'bare soil' throughout the observation period.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently devoid of living plant matter. The specimens are either buried, decomposed, or were never present in the observed pots.
anomalies: Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
Presence of a white rabbit figurine in P3 as the sole occupant.
vision_report.narrative_description: As a botanical observer, I have performed a multi-day audit of the provided image sequence. The registry provided (P1-P4) does not align with the physical reality of the images. Each pot contains only dark, uniform substrate. There is no evidence of String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Pothos, or Silver Guest. The only identifiable object is a white rabbit figurine in the P3 pot. The environment appears to be a static, non-biological setup or a failed cultivation attempt.
